rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] Movant shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order: Compelling Production Of Documents; Compelling Deposition Testimony; And Awarding Sanctions Memorandum In Support
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Mary Bong's Motion for Order: Compelling Production of Documents; Compelling Deposition Testimony; and Awarding Sanctions ("Motion"). The Motion seeks production of certain documents and ESI listed in the Requests for Production ("RFPs") served as part of the Amended Notice of Deposition of Annie Yi Tang in her capacity as an individual, Administrator of the Estate of James Won Ling Bong, co-trustee of the James Won Ling Bong Revocable Trust, co-trustee of the Fortune 8 Trust, and an officer of James Bong Enterprises, Inc., served on May 16, 2025. The Motion further seeks responses to certain questions regarding James Bong Enterprises, Inc.'s finances Betty Bong was asked at the June 19, 2025 deposition, to which her counsel raised oral objection and instructed her not to answer. The Motion is GRANTED, as follows. Annie Yi Tang, in her capacity as an individual, Administrator of the Estate of James Won Ling Bong, co-trustee of the James Won Ling Bong Revocable Trust, co-trustee of the Fortune 8 Trust and an officer of James Bong Enterprises, Inc., is ORDERED to produce, without further objections, Code-compliant production responsive to RFPs numbers 1 through 52 served as part of the Amended Notice of Deposition of Annie Yi Tang on May 16, 2025, within ten (10) days of service of Notice of Entry of this Order. Any privilege claims over responsive documents or ESI shall be reflected in a statutorily-compliant privilege log produced to the requesting party within ten (10) days of entry of this Order. Betty Bong, in her capacity as an individual, co-trustee of the James Won Ling Bong Revocable Trust, co-trustee of the Fortune 8 Trust and an officer of James Bong Enterprises, Inc. is ORDERED to respond to the questions concerning James Bong Enterprises, Inc.'s financial affairs asked at the June 19, 2025 deposition, as recorded in the deposition transcript at 106:22-109:10 and 109:25-112:3, attached as Exhibit F to the Motion's Separate Statement. Deposition to be resumed within ten (10) days of service of Notice of Entry of this Order. Movant' request for sanctions pursuant Code of Civil Procedure sections 2023.010(e) and 2023.030(a) is GRANTED. On the record currently before the court, there is no showing that Ms. Tang or Ms. Betty Bong acted with substantial justification in failing to respond to the specified RFPs or the deposition questions, or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust. Annie Yi Tang and Betty Bong are jointly and severally ORDERED to pay Mary Bong a total of $9,847.50 in monetary sanctions, within ten (10) days of service of Notice of Entry of this Order.
